items.title: The Cleveland Cavaliers signed Meleek Thomas, the 34th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, to a four-year, $9.3 million deal, league sources told HoopsHype. Thomas gets the first three years fully guaranteed for a total of $6.4 million. Agent Sam Permut of Roc Nation finalized the deal.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: The Cleveland Cavaliers signed Meleek Thomas, the 34th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, to a four-year, $9.3 million deal, league sources told HoopsHype. Thomas gets the first three years fully guaranteed for a total of $6.4 million. Agent Sam Permut of Roc Nation finalized the deal.

items.title: Andre Drummond is the last Sixers FA standing with any form of Bird rights.
items.author: Austin Krell
items.body_excerpt:
Andre Drummond is the last Sixers FA standing with any form of Bird rights.

Philly could’ve very well matched this and stayed below the hard cap.

Only questions now are whether they use the remainder of their MLE money or the bi-annual, or just sign minimums.

items.title: Fully guaranteed deal, no options, per source.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Fully guaranteed deal, no options, per source.

items.title: Kelly Oubre Jr. played with tremendous intensity for over 5,700 minutes during three years with the Sixers. For someone initially signed to a veteran's minimum deal right before training camp, Oubre gave this team a whole lot.

items.body_excerpt:
Kelly Oubre Jr. played with tremendous intensity for over 5,700 minutes during three years with the Sixers. For someone initially signed to a veteran's minimum deal right before training camp, Oubre gave this team a whole lot.

He has a chance to contend:
www.phillyvoice.com/sixers-news-...

media.title: Report: Kelly Oubre Jr. leaves Sixers for two-year, nearly $17 million contract with Indiana Pacers
media.description: In three seasons with the Sixers, Kelly Oubre Jr. averaged 32.1 minutes, 14.9 points, 5.4 rebounds and 1.3 steals across 178 games (150 starts).

items.title: Very reasonable deal for Oubre.
items.author: Derek Bodner
items.body_excerpt:
Very reasonable deal for Oubre. 

Since they had his Bird rights, Sixers could have offered Oubre the same contract, even after the Wade signing. Doing so would have taken the Sixers about $3m over the luxury tax line, but still well under the $209m hard cap.
